races.name: Return Of The Flat At Navan Handicap

runners.name: Distillate
runners.spotlight: Most recent of her six wins gained at Laytown last September; began the new campaign with a neck second over 7f at Dundalk; has failed to reproduce that form in two subsequent appearances and is now dropped in trip; placed only once from nine Navan runs.
runners.comment: Below her best twice since a Dundalk second, placed only once from nine Navan runs.
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel OHare & Philip Smith

runners.name: Platino Bianco
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 27 starts came at Newcastle in December on stable debut (6f); produced another good AW display when second in a 6f claimer at Dundalk a month ago, 66-1 then, reflecting the apparent scale of her task at the weights; has been hit with a 12lb rise.
runners.comment: Has been hit with a 12lb rise for an excellent run in a claimer, may find a few too good

runners.name: My Kurkum
runners.spotlight: No win since June 2022; twice runner-up in turf handicaps last May; three lesser efforts followed; well beaten in a 1m contest at Newcastle on first outing this year.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up in turf handicaps last May, four lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Exceeding
runners.spotlight: 1-27 record is off-putting but potentially interesting now after an excellent display from 6lb out of the handicap at Naas on his seasonal debut; kept on well to challenge the well-fancied winner Noble Nation with a useful yardstick in third; this track is similar in character to Naas.
runners.comment: Looks the part here after an excellent display from 6lb out of the handicap at Naas

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, no success since September 2022 and is now 33lb below his highest winning mark; had three different trainers last year and has changed stable again; perhaps some chance of a revival from this mark, but not a betting proposition
runners.comment: Last won in 2002; some chance of a revival from this mark but not a betting proposition

runners.name: Rattletheonionbag
runners.spotlight: A 29-race maiden; placed in six of her 17 races at Dundalk; unplaced at that venue recently following a better effort last month; once in the first three in 12 starts on turf.
runners.comment: A 29-race maiden. placed in six of her 17 races at Dundalk, turf form is unconvincing

runners.name: Happy Henry
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; put in a career-best when second of 19 over C&D last October; would have finished closer with a clear run at Dundalk on his next start; again met interference at that venue last time.
runners.comment: Career-best when second of 19 over C&D last Oct', not much luck in running at Dundalk

runners.name: Ukiyo
runners.spotlight: 6f winner for Gavin Cromwell on yielding ground at Cork last September; down the field over 7f on AW/stable debut at Dundalk in January; better over 6f last month but held by Rattletheonionbag.
runners.comment: 6f winner on yielding ground at Cork last September, held by Rattletheonionbag on AW form

runners.name: Alnofoor
runners.spotlight: Fair form for Natalia Lupini in 2021; back from over four years off when an encouraging fifth over C&D last October (Happy Henry, second); failed to build on that in two sprint handicap runs at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Fifth over C&D last October (Happy Henry, second); failed to build on that at Dundalk

runners.name: Apache Star
runners.spotlight: Most recent of his three wins (46 races) was in August 2023; on the plus side, he has a good place record (15 times); consistent at Wolverhampton in the early months of last year; ended his time in Britain with a poor Lingfield run last July; new yard.
runners.comment: Only three wins in a 46-race career but placed 15 times, lack of a recent run a drawback

runners.name: Never Shout Never
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with, as illustrated by 2-41 record; won 5f Dundalk handicap off 73 in December 2024; now down to 52, having been regressive since second in a claimer last summer; needs to step up on recent Dundalk form.
runners.comment: Regressive since second in a claimer last summer, needs to step up on recent Dundalk form

runners.name: Gegenpressing
runners.spotlight: Mid-field in four starts after winning at Sligo last May, his fifth win of a 58-race career; has a bit to find with Happy Henry (second), Alnofoor (fifth) and Distillate (sixth) on running over C&D last October.
runners.comment: Has a bit to find with Happy Henry and others on running over C&D last October

runners.name: Uncle Albert
runners.spotlight: Won well over 7f on heavy ground at Cork at the start of the 2024 season; has never managed to replicate that form; now 16lb below his winning mark; possible chance if the addition of cheekpieces makes a difference.
runners.comment: Won well over 7f on heavy ground in 2024, now 16lb below his winning mark, could feature

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: Heavy ground 5f winner in 2023, no success since; ran only twice last year, down the field in both starts in the autumn.
runners.comment: Heavy ground 5f winner in 2023, no success since, moderate form in two runs last year

runners.name: Zephron
runners.spotlight: Three-time Curragh winner; five wins in all, none since April 2024; weak form last season; has changed stable.
runners.comment: Has five wins to his name, none since April 2024; weak form last year, has changed stable

races.name: Vintage Crop Raceday April 25th (C & G) Maiden

runners.name: Jumpingthegun
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to winners Sirius Prospect (6f-1m Listed) and Bama Breeze (useful US 8.5f-10.5f), out of French 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; stablemate Battle Of Maldon appeals more.
runners.comment: Dam is a twice-raced half-sister to winners, stable's Battle Of Maldon is more appealing

runners.name: Matada
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns Y; second foal; dam French 7f winner (RPR 98), sister to French 5f/5.5f Group 3 winner Goken, half-sister to useful 1m/8.5f winner Hurricane Cloud; appeals on paper so check market.
runners.comment: Dam French 7f winner and a sister to French Group 3 sprint winner Goken, of some interest

runners.name: Tashakour
runners.spotlight: Well-related Night of Thunder colt; made encouraging Curragh debut last July, staying on late over 7f,  beaten little over 3l in sixth; failed to improve over the same course and trip the following month; may need further but experience should be an asset.
runners.comment: Encouraging Curragh debut last July staying on late over 7f, no progress on second run
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Battle Of Maldon
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Naas barrier trial last May; ran out of steam in the final furlong of a 7f maiden at Leopardstown the following month; reportedly scoped badly; 9/2 third-favourite then, market may provide clues.
runners.comment: Won 7f Naas barrier trial last May, had when well fancied an excuse at Leopardstown

runners.name: Zelani
runners.spotlight: Second foal; by New Bay out of a 1m2f winner, half-sister to winners Zannda (1m4f Group 3), Zanndabad and Zaynudin; good middle distance pedigree; hooded for debut; may need further.
runners.comment: Good middle-distance pedigree; hooded for debut; may need further than this
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Priorstown
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; brother to Listed-placed 1m2f winner Annerville, half-brother to 7f/1m AW winner Kodiellen; dam 2m winner, may need further in time.
runners.comment: Fifth foal. brother to Listed-placed 1m2f winner Annerville, might prefer further in time

runners.name: State Of Wyoming
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-brother to winners Terebellum (Group 2), Mount Wellington, and Miss Finland; dam 6f-1m winner (inc AW/Group 3); entered in Derby and Irish Derby; in good hands; should be ready to make an impact.
runners.comment: Ninth foal; half-brother to 1m2f Group 2 winner Terebellum, in good hands, merits respect

runners.name: Shaftesbury Avenue
runners.spotlight: $500,000 F, $1,000,000 Y; fifth foal; half-brother to Grade 1 winner Up To The Mar; dam unraced sister to a US 1m stakes winner Zapper Belle, out of US 7f Grade 1 winner; not bred for heavy ground but debut third at the Curragh provides much reassurance;  noteworthy that he is given another run relatively soon with cheekpiecess fitted.
runners.comment: Not bred for heavy ground but debut third at the Curragh provides much reassurance
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Fay Og
runners.spotlight: Equiano gelding, first foal out of a winning Fast Company mare; dam winning half-sister to winners Three Saints Bay and Barbill (Listed), out of unraced half-sister to useful 1m winner El Hayem; may need this initial experience.
runners.comment: From a respected source but will probably need to pick up experience

runners.name: The Rock Factor
runners.spotlight: Solid Flat pedigree; change of emphasis now after a Naas bumper run in January; appeared not to stay; concern now is that this might not be far enough for him.
runners.comment: Appeared not to stay in a bumper, concern now is that this might not be far enough for him

runners.name: Huffnpuff
runners.spotlight: Showed definite promise when keeping on from off the pace in a 1m Curragh maiden last October; Colin Keane booked.
runners.comment: Definite promise when keeping on from off the pace in a 1m Curragh maiden last October

runners.name: Majestic King
runners.spotlight: E320,000 yearling and 3,000gns Godolphin cast-off; well-related; down the field at Ballinrobe on only start last season; had plenty of horses behind in a 1m Curragh maiden on reappearance; handicap prospect
runners.comment: Had plenty of horses behind in a 1m Curragh maiden on reappearance, handicap prospect

runners.name: Monmouth
runners.spotlight: Improvement likely from mid-field Curragh debut over 6f; stablemate Shaftesbury Avenue is preferred.
runners.comment: Improvement likely from Curragh debut, stablemate Shaftesbury Avenue is preferred
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Only One Scobie
runners.spotlight: Has failed to beat a single rival in three Dundalk appearances at 6f/7f/1m.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a single rival in three Dundalk appearances at 6f/7f/1m.

runners.name: Sarangpur
runners.spotlight: Three undistinguished starts; some sort of chance in a handicap; up against it in a maiden
runners.comment: Three undistinguished starts. some sort of chance in a handicap, up against it in a maiden

races.name: Cusack Hotel Group Family Fun Day July 11th Handicap

runners.name: Taipan
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner has yet to score in a handicap; won a 1m6f conditions race at Galway for the second year running last August; below best in two runs since but goes well fresh, handles heavy ground and his yard won this last year; can't discount.
runners.comment: Five time winner, goes well fresh, yard won this last year, can't discount

runners.name: Mordor
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner scored off 8lb lower at Killarney last May (2m1f, good); solid second in 20.5f handicap at Goodwood (soft) in August; no impact in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket when last seen; not a great record fresh and testing ground another concern.
runners.comment: Won off 8lb lower at Killarney last year, not a great record fresh, testing ground a query

runners.name: Whats Your Game
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Cork (1m4f, soft) last April off 75; creditable second behind Carmers in a 1m5f listed race here (good) in May; 2m may have stretched him on return from a break at Dundalk last month; sharper for that and interesting back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Won h'cap debut at Cork off 17lb lower, placed at listed level, strip fitter for latest
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Star Harbour
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner didn't manage to get his head in front last year but ran well in defeat at times; capable off this mark but his record when fresh suggests he's likely best watched for this first run of the season.
runners.comment: Seven-time winner, no win last year, record suggests he's best watched on first run back

runners.name: Dawn Rising
runners.spotlight: No wins last season but ran some excellent races in defeat; finished fourth in the QR handicap at Galway, fifth in the Irish Cesarewitch and then signed off with a fine second in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket; course winner over hurdles but might just need this run.
runners.comment: Fine efforts in top h'caps last season, course winner over timber, might need this run

runners.name: Kizlyar
runners.spotlight: French winner (1m6f, gd-sft, RPR 86) has had three runs over hurdles since joining Joseph O'Brien, his best effort an 8.25l third at Punchestown (2m, sft-hvy) on latest; now makes his Flat handicap debut and the assessor hasn't taken any chances with his mark.
runners.comment: French winner, has been hurdling, handicap debutant, opening mark doesn't look a gimme

runners.name: Navy Waters
runners.spotlight: Won a Down Royal maiden (10.5f, good) win last May; added a 1m5f handicap at the same venue off 73 and then first past the post but demoted off 79 at Leopardstown (1m4f, heavy); outclassed in listed heat at the Curragh when last seen in November; could resume progress back in handicap company if fit for return.
runners.comment: First past the post three times last year, out of depth last time, go well if fit

runners.name: Jabbar
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 4-17 on turf on the Flat; last won on the AW over 2m in January 2025; beaten a neck off 89 at the Curragh (1m6f, good) in June; has had a recent spin over hurdles so racefit but might need a bit more help from the handicapper; blinkers on.
runners.comment: 4-17 on turf on the Flat, recent spin over hurdles but may be on tough mark, blinkers on

runners.name: Zoffman
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; won a maiden hurdle last April but losing run on the Flat stretches back to October 2022; has placed off higher marks in two previous Flat handicap runs here but not sure he wants very testing ground (well behind Navy Waters on heavy when last seen).
runners.comment: No win on Flat since Oct' 2022, has gone well here before but might prefer better ground

runners.name: Dippedinmoonlight
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; bumper winner got off the mark on the level in a C&D (soft) maiden in October; four runs over hurdles since so won't lack for fitness on Flat handicap debut but her opening handicap mark doesn't look overly generous.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, fit from hurdling, Flat handicap debutante, opening mark looks tough

runners.name: Weston
runners.spotlight: Two wins when trained in Germany but yet to get his head in front on these shores; handicapper giving him a chance but soundly beaten on last two starts over hurdles and on the Flat and needs to leave those efforts well behind; best watched after break.
runners.comment: German winner, winless on these shores, poor efforts last twice, watch after break
runners.owner: Thomas Cox/Nap Racing Syndicate/James M Nolan

runners.name: Galileo Dame
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; hacked up in a maiden hurdle here last month (2m, heavy); struggled on last three Flat starts in listed company in the autumn but might be a different proposition now tried in a handicap off what could be a workable mark; big player.
runners.comment: Won a maiden hurdle on heavy ground here latest, Flat handicap debutante, big player
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd/Sean ODriscoll/Middle Cow Umlimited

races.name: Enjoy Hospitality At Navan Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Whats New
runners.spotlight: Won at Roscommon (1m4f, soft) last September off 8lb lower; mostly campaigned over timber since but has failed to fire in that sphere the last twice on testing ground and similar conditions now back on the Flat.
runners.comment: 1m4f winner, struggled on heavy over hurdles last twice, similar conditions here

runners.name: Takarengo
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 3-39 on turf on the Flat but hasn't won a race of any description since September 2021and pulled up when last seen in a Thurles handicap chase in October; hasn't got a great record fresh and others appeal more.
runners.comment: No win since September 2021, pulled up over fences when last seen, others appeal more

runners.name: Kool One
runners.spotlight: C&D (yielding) winner in October 2024 off 11lb lower; won again over 1m4f at Roscommon (good) last August and added a Wexford maiden hurdle (good) 10 days later; out of sorts in four runs in both codes since then and ground probably not ideal here.
runners.comment: C&D winner, out of sorts since August wins, would probably prefer a sounder surface

runners.name: Sir Allen
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort has been off the track since a below par run over hurdles at Leopardstown in December 2024; best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, off the track since December 2024, best watched unless market speaks

runners.name: Earthsong
runners.spotlight: Best effort came on handicap debut over this C&D (yielding) last August, staying on from the back for a 2l third of 11 off this mark; things didn't go her way at the Curragh when last seen in October but she remains with potential; could go well if handling this ground (mixed messages in pedigree).
runners.comment: Good C&D run on h'cap debut, same mark now, has potential, go well if handles ground

runners.name: Young Churchill
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner (on good) is 0-9 on the Flat; best form has come in good ground maidens at Listowel (1m4f) and at the Curragh (1m2f); no impact in four starts on soft ground; needs the track to dry out.
runners.comment: Good ground hurdles winner, best Flat form on sound surface, needs track to dry out

runners.name: Granns Boy
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose and ground-versatile; mixed hurdling and the Flat to fine effect last year, winning three races (one on Flat off 5lb lower), and signed off with a close second over timber at Galway in October; won on his first run back last year and could still be on a nice mark on the level; big player.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose, ground-versatile, could still be on a nice mark, goes well fresh, player

runners.name: Bhean Saibhre
runners.spotlight: Generally dependable dual-purpose mare; stayed on well for a 4l third over extended 1m4f at Bellewstown (soft) when last seen in October; just 1lb higher now, this longer trip will suit and could go well if ready to rock after a break.
runners.comment: Dependable dual-purpose sort, stayed on well over 1m4f latest, go well if fit after break

runners.name: You Make Me Smile
runners.spotlight: Won four races last year, two over timber and two on the Flat, including her last start in this sphere at Galway in October (1m4f, heavy) off 11lb lower; outclassed in grade 3 novice hurdle here when last seen in November; career-best required off this mark and may need this.
runners.comment: Fine campaign last year, won last Flat start off 11lb lower, big career-best needed
runners.owner: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership

runners.name: Mystic Rose
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a whole lot in maidens and was soundly beaten on handicap debut at Galway when last seen in August; 5lb lower now but can only be watched.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on handicap bow at Galway in August, 5lb drop not enough to tempt

runners.name: Clifftop
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; had a recent run in a novice chase but all of his best form in both codes is on good ground and he's 8lb wrong here; yard has stronger contender with Grann's Boy.
runners.comment: Acts on good ground, 8lb wrong here, yard has better chance with Grann's Boy
runners.owner: O B P Carroll & Ralph Kelly & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Duty Bound
runners.spotlight: Two wins over jumps in 2025; last seen on the Flat in September 2024 when well beaten by Sir Allen off a mark of 70; just 3lb lower now and fell in a Wexford handicap chase when last seen in October; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: First run on Flat since Sept' 2024, fell in chase when last seen in Oct', others preferred
runners.owner: Desmond OBrien & John T ONeill & J P Flavin

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden (Smullen Series)

runners.name: Karrygrant
runners.spotlight: Excellent effort on debut at Leopardstown (7f, yld-sft) in October when beaten 0.75l into second (RPR 75); speedily bred but shaped the last day like further would suit; obvious chance if he stays.
runners.comment: Fine effort on debut at Leopardstown (7f), speedily bred, obvious chance if he stays

runners.name: Merciless
runners.spotlight: Raced exclusively over 1m on the AW and earned a rating of 65 after four starts; steps up in trip for her turf debut and she'll need to improve plenty to get involved here.
runners.comment: Rated 65 after four AW runs at 1m, needs to improve plenty on turf debut

runners.name: Sir Benji
runners.spotlight: Sottsass gelding; price rose from E15,500 as a yearling to E34,000 as a 2yo; half-brother to winners Boundless Power (5f; RPR 103), Andromedas Kingdom (1m-8.4f; 73), Saxon Kingdom (1m; 68) and Elegantly Bound (Greek 6f/7f); dam unplaced 7f-1m4f AW (63); local yard can ready one so check the market.
runners.comment: Sottsass gelding, half-brother to four winners, local yard can ready one, check market

runners.name: Honouramongthieves
runners.spotlight: Left form of first two runs behind when staying on well for second at Killarney in October (1m, sft-hvy), earning a rating of 75, and the winner of that landed a valuable auction race at Naas on her next start; pedigree suggests this trip will suit; each-way shout at least if ready after break.
runners.comment: Stayed on well over 1m at Killarney, this trip should suit, each way shout at least if fit

runners.name: Lord Aus
runners.spotlight: Australia gelding; E36,000 yearling; brother to 1m4f winner Denis Anthony (RPR 77), half-brother to winners Alphonse Le Grande (8.6f-2m2f inc 2yo/AW; 98), Lunigiana (5f AW; 79) and Illusion Of Time (bumper); dam 5f-7f winner (inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; 103); nice pedigree but Karrygrant look's yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Australia gelding, dam a Gr' 3 winner, nicely bred but Karrygrant looks yard's main hope

runners.name: Sweet Baby Zou
runners.spotlight: Zoustar filly; bought back for 30,000gns as a yearling; half-sister to winners Good American (7f 2yo/10.5f; RPR 105) and Falls Of Acharn (1m Flat, 83; 1m7f/2m hurdle); dam German 1m-1m4f winner (inc Group 3; 106); respected source and worth checking in the market.
runners.comment: Zoustar filly, dam German 1m-1m4f Group 3 winner, respected source, check market

runners.name: Cascade Canyon
runners.spotlight: Coulsty gelding was a 34,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Valiabad (French 9.5f/1m2f inc AW; RPR 103), Vanada (French 7.5f 2yo; 96), Valeya (French 1m4f) and Ruesga (Spanish 10.5f/1m3f); dam Listed-placed 9.5f AW/1m7f turf winner (104); makes some appeal on paper but look's yard's second string.
runners.comment: Coulsty gelding, dam Listed placed turf winner (RPR 104), looks yard's second string

runners.name: Mahzuz
runners.spotlight: King Of Change gelding; E6,000 yearling; dam unraced half-sister to winners Royal Eagle (1m4f/1m5f Flat and useful 2m hurdle), Pleasure Bent (1m-1m2f), Heho (1m2f) and Azhar (6f 2yo), out of unraced sister to Breeders' Cup Mile winner; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: King of Change gelding, dam unraced, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Trojan Fighter
runners.spotlight: Ulysses colt; 67,000gns yearling; half-brother to 1m 2yo/Qatari 1m3f winner Hornsea Bay (RPR 96); dam Listed-placed 1m2f/11.4f winner (99); likely type on paper and in good hands; holds an Irish Derby entry; no surprise to see a big run on debut.
runners.comment: Ulysses gelding, dam listed placed 1m2f winner, in good hands, could go well on debut

runners.name: Maxminelli
runners.spotlight: Came up short in two starts last year but did show a glimmer at Dundalk on latest when staying on late for a modest fourth over 1m; gelded since that run and will probably be seen to better effect when sent handicapping.
runners.comment: Modest form so far, gelded since last run at Dundalk, probably one for handicaps

runners.name: Forty Six A
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 11-1 on debut at the Curragh (7f, heavy) 15 days ago and showed up well before weakening late into an 11l fourth; not sure this longer trip will suit on that evidence.
runners.comment: Weakened late over 7f on debut at the Curragh, not sure this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Hippology
runners.spotlight: Kept on for a 5.25l fifth of 13 in a 1m maiden here last October when sent off at 10-1; bred to appreciate this longer trip and out of a Pivotal dam so should handle softer ground; holds Irish Derby entry; could go well.
runners.comment: Kept on well over 1m here on debut, bred for this longer trip, could go well

runners.name: Captain Con
runners.spotlight: Was always in rear when sent off at 100-1 on debut at the Curragh last July; has been gelded, steps up in trip and different ground here; probably best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: 100-1 and well beaten on debut last July, up in trip, gelded, watch unless market speaks

races.name: Book Your Navan Summer BBQ Packages Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Convincing
runners.spotlight: Placed three times; showed little over hurdles during the winter; left an impresssion he would benefit from a return to longer trips when a staying-on ninth of 26 over 1m at the Cuerragh; can do better but needs to reverse form with Satono Chevalier and Lady Mary Heath.
runners.comment: Fair run at the Curragh, needs to reverse form with Satono Chevalier and Lady Mary Heath

runners.name: Lisnadill
runners.spotlight: Best of four handicap runs last season when going close against an in-form rival over a little further than this at Naas in July; sent off favourite over C&D eight days later, dropped away in the final furlong, reported to have blood at both nostrils; has left Andy Oliver; first run for Colin McBratney.
runners.comment: Good chance based on Naas second last July, reported to have bled here eight days later

runners.name: Satono Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner at 1m4f; 0-12 on turf; coped well with his first exposure to heavy ground when third in a 26-runner contest at the Curragh; not much between him and Lady Mary Heath on that running; this longer trip will suit
runners.comment: Not much between him and Lady Mary Heath on Curragh running, this longer trip will suit

runners.name: Thru The Gap
runners.spotlight: Struggled in a claimer on the final 2025 start; does not appeal as well-handicapped; tongue-tie will need to make a difference
runners.comment: Does not appeal as well-handicapped, tongue-tie will need to make a difference

runners.name: Church Mountain
runners.spotlight: Beat today's rival Satono Cavalier over 1m4f at Dundalk in January, his ninth win in all and fourth at this venue; has found it tougher off higher marks since.
runners.comment: Beat Satono Cavalier at Dundalk in January, that rival has run well on turf since

runners.name: Acheron
runners.spotlight: Dam is an unraced half-sister to Irish Derby winner Westover; placed in two of his three Flat starts in France at three; ran creditably in a Dundalk handicap on only start last year; unseated at the second on recent hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Ran creditably in a Dundalk handicap on only 2025 start, unseated in a recent hurdle race

runners.name: Hosanna Power
runners.spotlight: 1m2f turf winner for Sir Michael Stoute in May 2024; has struggled in three Dundalk runs since joining this stable.
runners.comment: 1m2f turf winner for Sir Michael Stoute in May 2024, has struggled on AW for this stable

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Maiden on turf; only glimpses of worthwhile form last year; made some late headway when ridden by Colin Keane in a middle distance handicap at Dundalk earlier this month.
runners.comment: Made some late headway when ridden by Colin Keane at Dundalk, needs to find extra
runners.owner: Mrs M Cahill & Mrs Caroline Hendron & Peter Robin Parkhill

runners.name: Lady Plimsoll
runners.spotlight: Won Ballinrobe claimer at around this trip on good ground for John O'Donoghue; runner-up when upped in trip to an extended 1m4f at Thurles on second start for this stable; finished the season with second over 2m at Dundalk; an unknown quantity on this ground; handicap mark seems fair.
runners.comment: Stays further than this, an unknown quantity on this ground, handicap mark seems fair
runners.owner: Athlumney Farms Ltd & Eoin Baxter & Mrs M D OCallaghan

runners.name: Kevin The Great
runners.spotlight: Yet to reach the first three; was staying on at the finish at Naas on his final start last seasson; may need a bit further than this to make his presence felt
runners.comment: Yet to reach the first three, may need a bit further than this to make his presence felt

runners.name: Sayidah New Bay
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a pair of third placings in small-field races over further at Killarney and Musselburgh; this stiff 1m2f should bring stamina into play.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a pair of third placings in small-field races over further

runners.name: Lady Mary Heath
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form last year; made a promising start to the season when fourth of 26 over 1m at the Curragh; closely matched with Satono Cavalier who had the benefit of race fitness; however, that rival may be more effective at this longer trip.
runners.comment: Made a promising start to the season at the Curragh, closely matched with Satono Cavalier

runners.name: Anightaway
runners.spotlight: Weak form in turf maidens, no better on AW on handicap debut or over hurdles; beaten 22l when down the field at the Curragh on Saturday.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, in rear on AW on handicap debut, same again at Curragh on Saturday

runners.name: Calamint
runners.spotlight: Caused a 33-1 shock with 1m Naas handicap win on the first of two starts in 2024; failed to match that form in four starts last year; now 3lb below her winning mark; ability to run well when fresh is an asset.
runners.comment: Long-priced winner over on seasonal debut in 2024, failed to match that form last year

races.name: Bective Restaurant Handicap

runners.name: Pliny
runners.spotlight: Last two runs as a juvenile were over 6f; had better form over 7f last August/September; a big market drifter on his reappearance in a 7f claimer at Dundalk when second to a well-fancied rival; disappointing over 1m at the same venue on handicap debut; fair chance on overall form if he stays.
runners.comment: Disappointing run over 1m at Dundalk on handicap debut, fair 7f form, chance if he stays

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Showed mild promise in maidens and over 7f at Down Royal in her first nursery; regressive later in the season; plenty of improvement needed; wears a tongue-tie for the first time on her tenth start.
runners.comment: Showed mild promise in maidens and in her first nursery, regressive later in the season

runners.name: Accelereight
runners.spotlight: Struggled at two; finished last twice; gelded now; cheekpieces fitted; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Struggled at two, finished last twice, gelded now , cheekpieces fitted, hard to fancy
runners.owner: Any Excuse For A Holiday Syndicate

runners.name: Private Larry
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in three runs over different trips on turf and AW; his chance is not obvious.
runners.comment: Moderate form in three runs over different trips on turf and AW, his chance is not obvious

runners.name: Survivors Code
runners.spotlight: Nursery form ties in with Balance Of Trade; needs to find extra on the evidence of sixth placings at Gowran and Galway; has left Emmet Mullins to join Conor Maxwell who adds a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Form ties in with Balance Of Trade, needs to find extra, her new trainer fits a tongue-tie

runners.name: Elm Creek
runners.spotlight: Ordinary maiden form on turf in September/October last year; may step up from a Dundalk run with his fitness advanced and cheekpieces on; trainer has a senior rider on Celestially.
runners.comment: May step up from a Dundalk run, cheekpieces on, trainer has a senior rider on Celestially

runners.name: Dreoilin
runners.spotlight: Unfancied in two 1m runs at two, eighth of nine at Gowran (heavy) and ninth of 13 at Navan (yielding); took a step in the right direction when staying on for fourth in a 6f Curragh maiden on reappearance; by Teofilo out of a Dawn Approach mare, fair chance she will stay this far.
runners.comment: Two ordinary runs at two, took a step in the right direction in a 6f Curragh maiden

runners.name: Delusional
runners.spotlight: Long-priced second on AW at Southwell on second run for Jack Channon; Irish debut was inconclusive; gelded now; trainer also supplies the top weight.
runners.comment: Long-priced second on AW on second run for Jack Channon, Irish debut was inconclusive

runners.name: Celestially
runners.spotlight: Weak form on turf at two; no better on AW last month; premature to write off all the same; Gavin Ryan aboard with an apprentice on the stable's other runner.
runners.comment: Weak form on turf at two, no better on AW last month, premature to write off all the same

runners.name: Palace Tyne
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds and made no impact in good maidens at the Curragh (twice) and Leopardstown; not a bad run in a 1m claimer here on his final start for Jack Davison; could go well on stable/handicap debut
runners.comment: Not a bad run in a 1m claimer here on his final 2025 start, could go well on stable debut

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: Ran well on the first of two nursery outings, finishing in front of Survivor's Code when fourth of 15 at Gowran; failed to uphold form with that rival at Galway, though not much between them.
runners.comment: Made the frame in the first of two 7f nursery outings last year, handled heavy ground then

runners.name: Gold Find
runners.spotlight: Ran three times at Dundalk at two; possibly found 7f inadequate on his third start; previous run over 1m was respectable; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Possibly found 7f inadequate on his third AW start, previous run over 1m was respectable

runners.name: Servare
runners.spotlight: Low-key form in three runs in maidens; fair fourth of eight in a 7f Curragh nursery; mid-field over 1m at Naas; this longer trip may help.
runners.comment: Fourth of eight in a 7f nursery, mid-field over 1m at Naas, longer trip may help

races.name: Virgin Bet Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Barefoot Beach
runners.spotlight: Belied her 25-1 odds to win by 3l on her debut over C&D last September; she has a useful pedigree and holds strong claims under a 7lb penalty on this first outing since.
runners.comment: 3l win over C&D last September on her sole start; strong claims under a 7lb penalty

runners.name: Minnie Idol
runners.spotlight: 50-1 runner-up on debut at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) last month and fourth of 11 there (6f) 11 days ago when not helping her cause by being headstrong; there's every chance she'll be in the mix if settling better today.
runners.comment: 50-1 runner-up on debut last month then failed to settle when fourth of 11 last time

runners.name: Mehmas Engine
runners.spotlight: Cost 120,000euros at the breeze-ups last May and she was sent off odds-on when 2l third of nine on last month's debut at Southwell (5f, Tapeta); open to improvement now tackling 6f and could play a leading role.
runners.comment: 120,000euros breeze-up buy last May; promising third on debut and open to improvement

runners.name: Sizzling Seixas
runners.spotlight: Wore this hood when 12-1 for recent debut at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) and showed some promise in fourth of eight; this 130,000euros yearling is open to improvement if settling better and could be a contender.
runners.comment: 130,000euros yearling who is open to improvement if settling better on this second start

runners.name: Yasalam
runners.spotlight: 9-2 when fourth of six on debut at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) last August; absent since and her useful pedigree doesn't suggest she'll be a sprinter, but she showed good pace on that sole start; not ruled out; watch betting.
runners.comment: Showed good pace when fourth of six over 7f on sole 2yo start; not ruled out

races.name: Virgin Bet Interactive Handicap

runners.name: Southern Warrior
runners.spotlight: Clear promise in novice events and sent off favourite when a narrow winner last week at Newcastle on his handicap debut (6f); he did benefit from the stands' rail at Newcastle but has gone up only 3lb.
runners.comment: Benefited from the stands' rail last week at Newcastle but he's gone up only 3lb

runners.name: Zoustar Dreams
runners.spotlight: Not far away in 6f/5f maidens on Tapeta before her backward step here in December; those best efforts give her a chance off this mark and blinkers are tried.
runners.comment: Form of her two best runs gives her a chance off this mark; blinkers go on

runners.name: Staniel Cay
runners.spotlight: More consistent than progressive thus far (5f/6f) but goes handicapping off a low mark and may yet do better.
runners.comment: Goes handicapping off a low mark and may yet do better

runners.name: Escape Plan
runners.spotlight: Advanced his RPR with each run last season and appeared to perform below market expectations on handicap debut at Lingfield (6f; first-time tongue-tie) one month ago; that was after a break (and a gelding operation) and perhaps this track will suit better.
runners.comment: An improver last season and performed below market expectations on handicap debut

runners.name: Orange Emperor
runners.spotlight: His best run last season was on debut when fourth over 6f at Goodwood (hooded in his final race); has been gelded and his yard's patient approach offers hope for him making a better 3yo; market could be informative on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: His yard's patient approach offers hope for him making a better 3yo

races.name: Virgin Bet Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Holy Fire
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come here (6f/7f), the latest two starts ago; kept on for 2l fifth of 12 over C&D last time on her first crack at 1m and can better that performance when getting a stronger gallop, but there are doubts on that score this afternoon.
runners.comment: Promise over C&D on first crack at 1m; a strong gallop would help but she might not get it

runners.name: Jazzy Baby
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden in January on fourth start and she's run well to be third in C&D handicaps on both starts since; has the same mark as for those last two outings and could be thereabouts.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner; third over C&D in first two handicaps and she could be thereabouts

runners.name: Plaid
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of last October's debut at Nottingham to win over this C&D in December; she's in excellent hands to continue to progress and could play a leading role on this first handicap start.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in December on second start; in excellent hands to continue to progress

runners.name: Izznt She Hot
runners.spotlight: Short-headed at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last September on her third start; hasn't kicked on from that in her two outings since (handicap debut on the first occasion) but she has an excellent pedigree and is in top hands, and things may click at some point.
runners.comment: She needs to raise her game but excellent pedigree and things could click at some point

runners.name: Melody De Vega
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) last October on second start and similar form when third there (8.6f) later that month; cost 105,000euros in 2024 and could have more to offer; possible player on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed at Wolverhampton on final two 2yo starts; possible player on handicap debut
runners.owner: Michael P Tudor & J P M OConnor

runners.name: Art Gallery
runners.spotlight: Went close at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) last November on second start and won at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) in December; open to further improvement and pedigree suggests she'll be fine at 1m; respected on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in December on third start and she's respected on her handicap debut

runners.name: Touted Plan
runners.spotlight: Her 11 starts in Ireland for Joseph Murphy yielded one win, in a 1m maiden, and she was well beaten on her last two runs in the autumn; changed hands for 32,000gns in December; one to watch in the betting on stable debut.
runners.comment: 1-11 in Ireland; changed hands for 32,000gns in December; check betting on stable debut

races.name: Virgin Bet Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Shushi
runners.spotlight: Unable to pose a serious threat when fourth over 1m on turf last August/September on her first two starts; however, her dam was a Group 2 winner (1m) and she's by Camelot, so this step up in trip could prompt improvement on her reappearance; yard going well and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: By Camelot and could improve upon her 1m 2yo form now up in trip on her reappearance

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros foal, 80,000euros yearling; ninth foal; half-sister to five winners including Taamol (6f-1m including Listed), Botas (6.5f-8.5f including AW; RPR 88) and Queen Of Seduction (11.6f/1m4f; RPR 87); dam half-sister to Italian Oaks winner; makes debut with yard in good form; a possible.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five winners; makes debut with yard in good form and she's a possible

runners.name: Galaxy Star
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper on debut at Stratford last October and went close at Newbury in December; soundly beaten in Listed bumper at Market Rasen in January; this Flat-bred 4yo could go well on her first outing in this code.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who has a Flat pedigree and could go well on first run in this code

runners.name: Aukus
runners.spotlight: 115,000gns yearling; eighth foal; sister to 1m 2yo/1m2f winner Herring Island (RPR 102), closely related to Group-placed 7f 2yo winner War Rooms (106), half-sister to two winners; with a top yard and could go well on debut.
runners.comment: 115,000gns yearling with attractive pedigree; with a top yard and could go well on debut

runners.name: Semele
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in bumpers and this half-sister to five Flat winners who may be suited by the switch to this code; however, Galaxy Star had her measure in bumpers so improvement is certainly needed on her Flat debut.
runners.comment: Showed ability in bumpers but Galaxy Star had her measure in that sphere; Flat debut
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs A E Pakenham

runners.name: Six Blue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at big odds on all three bumper starts and a transformation is necessary now starting out on the Flat.
runners.comment: Tailed off at big odds on all three bumper starts; transformation necessary on Flat debut

runners.name: Liveinthelight
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at 50-1 at Ascot (1m, soft) last September on her sole start; however, it's very early days for this 290,000gns yearling and it will be interesting to see if she's stronger in the betting this time.
runners.comment: Tailed off at 50-1 last September on sole start; however, early days & she cost 290,000gns
runners.owner: Lady Bamford Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster

runners.name: Shirakawa
runners.spotlight: Well bred and she could appreciate this step up in trip on her second start; however, she was beaten a long way at 33-1 on debut here (1m) in January and it's hard to be confident on the back of that.
runners.comment: Well bred and this longer trip can suit but she needs to leave her debut form miles behind

runners.name: Calypso Bloom
runners.spotlight: 125-1 when down the field on belated debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) in December; this 5yo needs to be a different proposition today.
runners.comment: 125-1 when down the field on belated debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) in December

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap

runners.name: Our Dagger
runners.spotlight: 5yo who has had several different stables during his light career; well beaten at Galway last autumn on final two outings but he's well worth a second look today given he's 2-2 on AW, the latest of those wins coming over C&D last February; makes stable debut with new trainer's horses running well.
runners.comment: Struggling in Ireland last autumn but 2-2 on AW and new trainer's horses are running well

runners.name: Me Tarzan
runners.spotlight: Won on stable debut at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) in December and back near that level when 1l fourth there (7f) ten days ago; also effective over this C&D and could have a big part to play under regular rider Harry Vigors.
runners.comment: Close fourth at Lingfield ten days ago and he's also effective over this C&D; contender

runners.name: Straight A
runners.spotlight: Beaten on his last 14 starts going back to last January but he's often run well in that time, including over C&D and when a very close fourth at Newcastle in February last time; there's every chance he'll be bang there.
runners.comment: Beaten on last 14 starts but often runs well and there's every chance he'll be bang there

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: 0-13 in handicaps since winning a maiden at Newcastle in November 2024; however, he's produced positive performances on his last two starts and could be in the mix off a career-low mark.
runners.comment: 0-13 in handicaps but ran pretty well last time and has a career-low mark

runners.name: Lunanova
runners.spotlight: Won three times, the latest two for the Newland/Insole yard, at Brighton last summer; however, she was below her best in the autumn and sold for 3,500gns in October; revival required on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Three wins at Brighton last summer but below best in the autumn; makes stable debut

runners.name: Shamacid
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who was down the field in January in his first two handicaps but he's been gelded since and is on a competitive mark on his best novice/maiden form; not ruled out in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Down the field in first two handicaps but gelded since and not ruled out in a tongue-tie

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Extra Places Handicap

runners.name: South Coast Star
runners.spotlight: She's improved with each of her three starts in handicaps and went close at Wolverhampton (7f, Polytrack) a fortnight ago; showed promise in a novice here in December and Harry Vigors removes a valuable 7lb; player.
runners.comment: Went close at Wolverhampton a fortnight ago and she's effective here; player

runners.name: Archangel Josepi
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who is unraced on Polytrack but he ran well when second at Newcastle (7f) this month, when denied a clear run, and can be in the mix if backing up that performance.
runners.comment: 0-11 but runner-up at Newcastle last time and in the mix if backing up that performance

runners.name: Quick Quasar
runners.spotlight: He has a useful pedigree and, having been gelded, he showed improved form when going close in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on handicap debut at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last Thursday; strong claims.
runners.comment: Went close on handicap debut at Southwell last Thursday and holds strong claims

runners.name: Denbys Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but not beaten far in 6f handicaps on the Lingfield Polytrack on her last two starts and she's shaped each time as though the return to 7f would be a positive; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Has been shaping over 6f at Lingfield as though this return to 7f will be a positive

runners.name: Prancy Peer
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish better than fifth across her six starts but when filling that position at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) last time she shaped as though the return to 7f would help; 3lb lower today and not discounted.
runners.comment: 0-6 but weakened when fifth over 1m at Lingfield last time; not ruled out now back at 7f

runners.name: Sail On Sailor
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced half-brother to five winners who is one to watch in the betting now starting out for a new yard; however, he was soundly beaten on his handicap debut and final run for Brett Johnson at Chelmsford (6f, Polytrack) in November when last seen and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Lightly raced & market check advised on stable debut but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Amber Hamur
runners.spotlight: Her first three starts were over 7f last autumn (including C&D) and improvement is needed on this handicap debut; however, she's bred to be better than this opening mark; interesting, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Improvement needed but she's bred to be better than this opening mark; interesting
runners.owner: G C Myddelton & V E C Taylor

runners.name: Superstorm
runners.spotlight: Mixed form in winless, six-race 2yo campaign but he ran well when third at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) in November two starts ago; gelded since latest start and not ruled out on this return to action.
runners.comment: Inconsistent, winless 2yo campaign but returns having been gelded and is not ruled out

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Montu
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but 1l second at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) last month and filled the same position at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) subsequently, again keeping on well; might not be far away now up in trip in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Has kept on for second over 9.4f/1m2f on last two starts; might not be far away

runners.name: Blackisthenewblack
runners.spotlight: She's raced three times and has shown ability at up to 1m; her pedigree doesn't scream 1m4f performer but she's nevertheless one to consider now up in trip on handicap debut for her in-form, respected stable.
runners.comment: Has shown ability at up to 1m; one to consider now up in trip on h'cap debut; yard in form
runners.owner: Team Valor Cayton Park Stud A Singh

runners.name: Private Project
runners.spotlight: Kept on nicely for 3l third of seven on the Lingfield Polytrack this month when upped to 1m2f, and today's longer trip is well worth a go; however, she's 0-7 and takes on some unexposed types; may again be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Kept on nicely over 1m2f last time but now 0-7 and takes on some unexposed types today

runners.name: Salkadan
runners.spotlight: Unable to threaten across his first three starts but those were over 7f/6f and this Ulysses gelding is tackling a vastly different trip on handicap debut; interesting for top jumps yard, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Upped markedly in trip for h'cap debut; interesting for top jumps yard; watch the betting

runners.name: Kindly Queen
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability across her first three starts and the step up to 1m4f could prompt improvement from this Nathaniel filly on handicap debut; in top hands and unraced dam is from a classy family.
runners.comment: The step up to 1m4f could prompt improvement from this Nathaniel filly on handicap debut

runners.name: Rum Diamond
runners.spotlight: Limited promise over 1m late last year on first three starts but gelded since and this half-brother to connections classy pair Burdett Road and East India Dock could be an entirely different proposition now upped in trip on handicap debut, for which he wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Classy pedigree indicates he could be different proposition now up in trip on h'cap debut

runners.name: Darn Hot Dame
runners.spotlight: Favourite when upped in trip and 2l third of nine on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, Tapeta) last month, and her attractive pedigree suggests she'll improve again for today's further increase in distance.
runners.comment: Third on handicap debut; pedigree suggests she'll improve again now upped further in trip

runners.name: Cospicua
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Newcastle (1m4f) last month but she's possibly unsuited by Tapeta and two starts ago she made a winning handicap debut at 25-1 over this C&D; not written off now back here.
runners.comment: Won on handicap debut over C&D and possibly unsuited by Tapeta at Newcastle since

runners.name: Wedonttellthetruth
runners.spotlight: Improved upon his three winter novice runs (9.4f/8.6f) when winning in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) three weeks ago; did it nicely and could have more to offer at the distance; respected.
runners.comment: Won when upped to 1m4f on handicap debut and could have more to offer at the distance

runners.name: Jambearee
runners.spotlight: Dam was a useful 1m winner; goes up in trip on handicap debut and he's one to watch in the betting off a lowly mark, but he's achieved little across his first three starts (6f-1m) and a big step forward is necessary.
runners.comment: Up in trip on handicap debut; watch the betting, but he's achieved little thus far

runners.name: Moon Is Up
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way when 20-1 for handicap debut here (1m) in November; however, she showed promise at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) previously and the dam's side of her pedigree hints she may relish this longer trip.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 1m here on h'cap debut but it's possible she'll relish this longer trip

races.name: Weatherbys nhstallions.co.uk Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jimbo Sport
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who wasn't overly pressed to justify favouritism in a 2m1f maiden at Newcastle last month (good to soft; has won on good) and looks sure to put up another bold show under a penalty.
runners.comment: Newcastle winner over 2m1f who looks sure to put up another bold show under penalty

runners.name: Scarlet Moon
runners.spotlight: Useful four-time Flat winner at up to 1m6f; the penalty contributed to his undoing at Stratford last time (2m2f, soft) but he's proving consistent over hurdles and won't mind getting back on less testing ground; can lead.
runners.comment: Consistent over hurdles and he won't mind getting back on less testing ground; can lead

runners.name: Snatch A Glance
runners.spotlight: Both wins on this course, a bumper on good to firm and a good-ground maiden in November; clear form claims even with a penalty and his yard won this last year.
runners.comment: Clear form claims even with a penalty and his yard won this last year

runners.name: Kings Scholar
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins for Ed Walker (1m4f/1m5f) and was consistent until getting too high in the weights; should enjoy good ground on hurdle/stable debut and he's of clear interest.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner who will stay the trip now hurdling and he's of useful ability
runners.owner: Paddy Brennan Racing Syndicate King S

runners.name: Current Edition
runners.spotlight: Close up when falling late on in an Irish point won by the smart Love Sign d'Aunou; bumper form is fairly underwhelming but he's had a wind operation and should do better over hurdles.
runners.comment: Irish point run was promising; bumper efforts less so but has had a wind op; hurdle debut

runners.name: Josh Central
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden with weak form claims and handicaps will be more like it.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden with weak form claims and handicaps will be more like it

runners.name: Kenobi
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a bumper (good to soft) and pulled up in a Hereford novice (soft), going off long odds on both occasions.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a bumper (good to soft) and pulled up in a Hereford novice (soft)

runners.name: Golden Garden
runners.spotlight: 1m6f AW winner for Jamie Osborne; tailed off on hurdle debut at Taunton in December (good; 12-1) but reportedly made a noise and has since had wind surgery; others are still preferred.
runners.comment: 1m6f AW winner; had a wind operation since quiet hurdle debut in December

races.name: G.C.Rickards Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Kits Coty
runners.spotlight: Hasn't kicked on from her win over 3m here on good ground in October but she's back on that winning mark and she had hurdle form at about this trip.
runners.comment: Back on the mark she defied over 3m here in October and couldn't rule out

runners.name: Annie Express
runners.spotlight: Making stable debut when scoring comfortably over 3m here in January (good to soft) but has to get back on track after three lesser efforts (the latest in a first-time visor).
runners.comment: Course winner in January but has to get back on track after three lesser efforts

runners.name: Lynsey Larue
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner; having only her fourth run over fences when runner-up at Warwick (3m, soft) and looks up to winning races off this low a mark; good ground will be fine.
runners.comment: Second at Warwick last time and looks up to winning races off this low a mark

runners.name: A Little Something
runners.spotlight: Competitive over hurdles last spring and heavy ground was a feasible excuse when pulled up on chase debut at Leicester in February after 281 days off (had lost position when sprawling at the 11th); can be given another chance.
runners.comment: Competitive on best hurdling form; heavy ground an excuse when pulled up on chase debut

runners.name: Moonlightinthepark
runners.spotlight: Some promise over hurdles, although didn't raise her profile in two recent handicaps over 2m5f and 2m1f; did finish second in her only point so chasing could be the making of her.
runners.comment: Modest hurdler but second in her only point so chasing could be the making of her

runners.name: Wishful Wings
runners.spotlight: Beaten about 12l at Warwick (3m, soft) on chase/handicap debut, finishing behind Lynsey Larue.
runners.comment: Beaten 12l at Warwick (3m, soft) on chase/handicap debut, finishing behind Lynsey Larue

races.name: Ben Chambers Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: DJo Dela Barriere
runners.spotlight: Won 3m handicap hurdle at Ffos Las (good) in October and followed up in a chase here over the same trip later that month when finishing alone; form has dipped since, over fences and hurdles; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Form has dipped since hurdle/chase wins in October and needs to bounce back

runners.name: Little Pi
runners.spotlight: Tended to hang when failing favourite backers off this mark here (2m5f) last month but that was another solid enough run; should be heavily involved.
runners.comment: Beaten off this mark here one month ago but that was another solid run

runners.name: Jackomy
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo; well backed and scored comfortably at Wincanton (2m4f, good) last week and looks ahead of his mark, even with a penalty.
runners.comment: Won easily last week and looks ahead of his mark, even with a penalty

runners.name: Oakleys Way
runners.spotlight: 2m3f winner at Taunton in November (good to firm) and possibly better again when beaten by his market rival with the pair clear at Plumpton (2m4f, good to soft) this month; up 3lb but looks a strong candidate today.
runners.comment: Beaten by market rival with the pair clear at Plumpton (2m4f); up 3lb; strong candidate

runners.name: Triple Haych
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point but has achieved precious little over hurdles (2m-2m3f) and has a lot to prove on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Achieved precious little over hurdles and has a lot to prove on this handicap debut

runners.name: Kates Choice
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who was well beaten on both starts (2m4f maiden/2m5f handicap) for new stable in the autumn; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden who was well beaten on both starts for new stable in the autumn

races.name: Weatherbys Racing Bank Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Your Darling
runners.spotlight: Showed he stays 3m when only just missing out at Kempton in January (good) where he would probably have won but for fluffing the last fence; another good effort at Ascot (3m) since, when coming from a long way off the pace to take third; strong contender off his competitive mark.
runners.comment: Exellent runs over 3m the last twice and he's a srong contender off his competitive mark

runners.name: Beachcomber
runners.spotlight: Appeared to get his campaign back on track when third over hurdles at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) and, when a 10l fifth over C&D since, he was softened up by getting competitive a good way out; he doesn't have a convincing profile but his pure ability is respected.
runners.comment: He doesn't have a convincing profile but his pure ability is respected

runners.name: Destroytheevidence
runners.spotlight: Took the notable scalp of Haiti Couleurs on his chase debut last season and ran well afterwards, including a near miss in this race; poor the last twice (heavy/soft) when tried in a visor but the cheekpieces that have worked well for him are now back on; much respected.
runners.comment: Ran well here in December and the return of cheekpieces is a definite positive

runners.name: Annsam
runners.spotlight: Won this race three years ago and gained his first win since when drawing clear over 3m1f back here in December; since then he's suffered a heavy fall at Sandown and an unfortunate early unseat at Kempton but respected back here as he could still be in fair form.
runners.comment: Two non-completions to bounce back from but he's a former winner of this race

runners.name: Lounge Lizard
runners.spotlight: Potentially well treated on old form but signed off for Henry Daly with his own ideas about the game and he made a quiet start for this yard at Huntingdon after wind surgery; risky.
runners.comment: He made a quiet start for this yard at Huntingdon after wind surgery; risky

runners.name: The Kalooki Kid
runners.spotlight: Won last season's Scottish Champion Chase (2m4f, good to soft; his last win) off 5lb lower but only sixth in the same race when last seen two months ago and there were no clear excuses; this is a drop in grade but has never given the impression that he's a stayer in the making (unraced beyond 2m5f).
runners.comment: Down in class but unraced beyond 2m5f so there are major stamina concerns

races.name: Parkinsons Awareness Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: High Fibre
runners.spotlight: Classy on the Flat back in the day; it had been a struggle in handicaps this season but, back from a break and dropped in grade, he won readily enough under Ciaran O'Shea at Kempton (2m, good) last week; escapes a penalty.
runners.comment: No penalty for last week's comfortable Kempton win when back from a break
runners.owner: G C Stevens And Mrs C Fry

runners.name: Roysse
runners.spotlight: Winner of bumper (soft) and maiden hurdle (2m, good to soft); his free-going tendencies have remained evident over fences (0-4) but he might be happier back hurdling and this track should suit his style if they do go forward with him.
runners.comment: Free-goer who is 0-4 over fences; back over hurdles on a track that could suit

runners.name: D Day Arvalenreeva
runners.spotlight: 3-9 over hurdles but was tailed off in her final jumps run in November (2m2f, good) and she's been good and bad on the AW since then; C&D winner who is on a realistic mark but she is becoming tough to predict.
runners.comment: C&D winner on a realistic mark but she is becoming tough to predict

runners.name: Jack Hyde
runners.spotlight: Three-time novice winner on good to soft who has performed admirably in Graded and handicap company; didn't run much of a race in the Imperial Cup but he was single-figure odds and this wouldn't be as demanding as that Sandown race; tongue-tie removed; respected.
runners.comment: Not handicapped out of it and this is easier than latest run in the Imperial Cup
runners.owner: Paddy Brennan Racing Syndicate Jack Hyde

runners.name: Crest Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who was well on top late on to win a Taunton maiden on good ground in January; highly likely that heavy ground didn't float his boat when tailed off back there under a penalty; doesn't look hurled in for this first handicap but he wouldn't be exposed yet.
runners.comment: Doesn't look hurled in for this first handicap but wouldn't be exposed yet

runners.name: Filibustering
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle wins on soft and good to soft; still early days over hurdles but he was bitterly disappointing in his first handicap, at Kempton two months ago; others look safer.
runners.comment: Juvenile hurdle winner but bitterly disappointing in his first handicap two months ago

races.name: Bob Cashmore & Michael Davies Open Hunters' Chase

runners.name: Jet Plane
runners.spotlight: Still in front when falling on his return to hunter chasing in January but ran miserably at Leicester next time where soft ground was no excuse; had been in good form in handicaps before those setbacks and would take some beating if back on song.
runners.comment: Running well in handicaps before his recent setbacks in hunter chases; class respected

runners.name: Northern Bound
runners.spotlight: It was a match he won at Warwick in October (2m4f handicap, good) but that took his strike-rate to 9-30 over fences; remains in the same colours despite leaving Ben Pauling; should give his running on hunter chase debut.
runners.comment: Handicap winner in October, taking his strike-rate over fences to 9-30; hunter chase debut

runners.name: Great Valley
runners.spotlight: Has a mountain to climb on BHA ratings but his was earned over hurdles and he's been going great guns in points during the past 12 months or so; it's also worth bearing in mind that in late 2024 he got to within 3l of this month's Cheltenham Festival hero Barton Snow in a 3m point; only a 6yo and could run well.
runners.comment: Modest hurdler but he's not without hope given his subsequent point-to-point form

runners.name: Missed Tee
runners.spotlight: 0-13 over regulation fences and she needs to improve on her recent seconds in hunter chases at Stratford and here.
runners.comment: She needs to improve on her recent seconds in hunter chases at Stratford and here

runners.name: Fix At All
runners.spotlight: Won this in 2023; somewhat resurgent at Catterick this month but in a weaker race than this and he was beaten fair and square in a match at Kempton last week.
runners.comment: His third at Catterick was in a weaker race; beaten in a match last week; unconvincing

races.name: Midnite A Next Generation Betting App Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Instant Bond
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at 18th attempt when scoring at Newcastle earlier this month (6f) and fair third there since; top amateur rides; one with a chance.
runners.comment: Newcastle winner earlier this month; top amateur rides; one with a chance
runners.owner: David Tate Danny Lawson & Kevin Broadley

runners.name: Forever Noah
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when winning at Chelmsford in February (6f); creditable efforts over 5f here and over 6f at Southwell on next two starts but has lost his form since.
runners.comment: Chelmsford winner in February; good efforts next two runs but has since lost his form

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; record of 2-40, those wins coming over 1m1f/1m on turf in 2023; third over 9.5f here and over 1m2f at Lingfield in January but below form since; quite interesting down markedly in trip in a weak contest; blinkers replace a visor.
runners.comment: Two wins have been at 1m1f/1m; interesting down markedly in trip; blinkers replace visor

runners.name: Yorkstone
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 7f classified stakes at Newcastle 11 days ago when wearing cheekpieces for the first time; below best in two previous runs over this trip (has won at 1m).
runners.comment: Won 7f Newcastle classified in cheekpieces last time; return to 6f not sure to suit

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D classified stakes earlier this month and fair third off this mark in a C&D handicap next time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Won C&D classified two runs back; fair third in C&D handicap last week; each-way chance

runners.name: Spun To Gold
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year, three on AW (6f/7f); some fair form since but has a bit to find with Instant Bond on recent form at Newcastle (6f); 4lb lower than last winning mark; first run here.
runners.comment: Three AW wins last year (6f/7f) and some fair form since; behind Instant Bond last time

runners.name: Chiedozie
runners.spotlight: 23-race maiden; has a chance on his C&D third in January 2025 but then off for 410 days; creditable 4.75l tenth of 12 at Kempton on his return to action this month; each-way chance.
runners.comment: 0-23; chance on his C&D third in January 2025; off 410 days since but fair run on return

runners.name: Eldeyaar
runners.spotlight: Has won three times over C&D; good fourth here in February and had excuses for a lesser run here last time; well drawn; one to consider despite being 1lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner; good C&D fourth in February and excuses for lesser run latest; chance

runners.name: No Gain
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 7f handicap in Jersey last June (firm); unraced since; plenty to prove back at 6f on stable debut with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Winner in Jersey last June (7f); plenty to prove at 6f on stable debut; cheekpieces added

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden; has a fair bit to find on recent efforts and his high draw is also a negative; others stronger.
runners.comment: 0-13; plenty to find on recent efforts; high draw a negative; others appeal more

runners.name: Conquest Of Power
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins in February 2025 (6f/1m); inconsistent since, best run when third in a classified event over C&D two runs back (well beaten over 1m since); a possible on best efforts.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in February 2025; mainly below form since, best run when 3rd in C&D classified

runners.name: Noble Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Ex-Andrew Balding; yet to hit form for current yard and best watched at present.
runners.comment: Ex-Andrew Balding; yet to hit form for this yard and is best watched at present

runners.name: Hallowed Time
runners.spotlight: Won a Chester handicap on heavy ground in September 2024 but mainly below form since (finished last of seven over C&D last week); well handicapped but has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Chester winner in September 2024; mainly below form since; good mark but plenty to prove

races.name: Make The Move To Midnite Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sovereign Spell
runners.spotlight: Passed the post in front in 5f Windsor novice last August but disqualified on technical grounds; better effort when third in a Doncaster nursery (6f, good) in September off a mark of 85; the one to beat on AW debut.
runners.comment: Showed enough last season to suggest he will take all the beating on his AW debut here

runners.name: Royal Poetry
runners.spotlight: By Lope De Vega; 15-2 when third of 13 in a 1m Southwell novice on debut in November; should do better and is a possible but drop in trip not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Third in a 1m Southwell novice on debut; should improve but drop in trip not sure to suit
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Gris De Chine
runners.spotlight: By Havana Grey; 40-1 when 4.25l fifth of eight in a C&D novice three weeks ago; improvement likely.
runners.comment: 40-1, creditable fifth of eight in a C&D novice three weeks ago; improvement likely

runners.name: The Jackal
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling who was sold for 16,000gns in November; gelded half-brother to two winners (6f/7f, one an AW winner) out of a 5f winner; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling; 16,000gns 2yo; half-brother to 2 winners; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Cosmic Jive
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns foal; by Starman; gelded half-brother to three winners including Group-placed 6f/7f 2yo; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: 32,000gns foal; by Starman; half-brother to three winners; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Jubilee Time
runners.spotlight: Ordinary efforts so far and was beaten 13l when third of five at Kempton (6f) last time; looks one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Ordinary efforts so far and looks more one for handicaps later in the year

runners.name: Romeos Guardian
runners.spotlight: Makes a quick reappearance after finishing 13l ninth of 11 after a slow start at Chelmsford four days ago (6f, 12-1); much more needed.
runners.comment: Quick comeback after poor debut effort at Chelmsford last week (slowly away); more needed

runners.name: Noble Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in two 6f maidens at Newcastle this year; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Modest efforts in two 6f maidens at Newcastle; others look stronger

runners.name: Lightning Hooves
runners.spotlight: Has been gelded since two poor 7f runs on the AW last autumn; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Has been gelded since two poor AW runs over 7f last autumn; best watched for now

races.name: Midnite Aint Your Grandads Bookie Handicap

runners.name: Boubyan
runners.spotlight: Running well in Class 5s and not beaten far in a bunched finish last time at Newcastle (1m; fine over further); he is 0-7 in Class 4 races, though.
runners.comment: In form as he wasn't far away when fifth last time; however, he's 0-7 in Class 4 races

runners.name: Patrol
runners.spotlight: Has won at 1m but last two wins have been over 1m4f and his form can be up and down.
runners.comment: Has won at 1m but last two wins have been over 1m4f and his form can be up and down

runners.name: Kings Hand
runners.spotlight: Bought for 32,000gns after winning over 1m2f at Lingfield in January for the Prescott yard; it's been a solid start for new connections (1m2f/1m3f) and he was a bit too free last time in front, but this will be his first crack at a Class 4 handicap.
runners.comment: Pleasing start for new yard but this will be his first crack at a Class 4 handicap

runners.name: Ernies Valentine
runners.spotlight: Seven wins on the AW; consistent in the main and ran okay over the 8.6f here ten days ago, leaving the impression that this longer trip is worth another go.
runners.comment: Running well and gives the impression that this longer trip is worth another go

runners.name: Serious Look
runners.spotlight: It was a narrow verdict over 8.6f here four weeks ago and he went up only 3lb; with that run under his belt and being a triple C&D winner, he should feature again if able to overcome a wider than ideal draw.
runners.comment: Another win here four weeks ago; every chance if overcoming his wide draw

runners.name: Tribal Wisdom
runners.spotlight: 4-8 at Wolverhampton and never ideally positioned in a slowly run race here 17 days ago; never ruled out around here.
runners.comment: 4-8 at Wolverhampton and never ideally positioned in a slowly run race here 17 days ago

runners.name: Criminal
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who hasn't shone since returning from a break and best trip remains questionable.
runners.comment: Maiden who hasn't shone since returning from a break and best trip remains questionable

runners.name: Lordsbridge Blu
runners.spotlight: Novice winner who doubled his tally by a head over Lingfield's 1m2f in November; could go well off 3lb higher if ready to rock after his break.
runners.comment: Novice winner who doubled his tally by a head over Lingfield's 1m2f in November

runners.name: Al Durry
runners.spotlight: Went close here last May and 154 days later she quickened up best off slow fractions in a 1m Southwell maiden; should do better over this longer trip and Owen Burrows remains her trainer despite the sale in December by former owners for 95,000gns.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap debutante who won a Southwell maiden when last seen

runners.name: Arcturus Flame
runners.spotlight: It was over 1m3f when he went down narrowly at Kempton on final run in early December but he's effective at this sort of trip and hasn't had many chances on the AW; couldn't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Near miss over 1m3f on final run in December but he's effective at this trip

runners.name: Annexation
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner but a 10yo now and opposable on this winter's exploits.
runners.comment: An eight-time winner but a 10yo now and opposable on this winter's exploits

runners.name: Nedita
runners.spotlight: Dual Dundalk winner at 1m; left Ireland out of form (changed hands for 6,000gns) and he was tailed off at big odds for stable debut at Southwell.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner in Ireland; tailed off at big odds for stable debut at Southwell

runners.name: Dawn Of Liberation
runners.spotlight: York winner over 1m2f last summer off 4lb lower and a few good runs afterwards before losing his way; needs a revival and he's 0-8 on AW surfaces.
runners.comment: Multiple turf winner but needs a revival and he's 0-8 on AW surfaces

races.name: Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Bergamo Gold
runners.spotlight: In-form filly who has won at Lingfield (1m2f) and over C&D on her last two starts; 4lb higher than last win; should give another good account.
runners.comment: Has won her last two starts, latest off 4lb lower over C&D; should go well again

runners.name: Wind Summer
runners.spotlight: Gelded after running poorly on handicap debut at Kempton in January (1m); second in Chelmsford maiden over 1m last time when shaping as though this step up in trip would suit; interesting.
runners.comment: Gelded after poor run on h'cap debut in January; maiden 2nd latest (1m); 9.5f should suit

runners.name: Pangbourne
runners.spotlight: Improved on her maiden/novice form when length second to an in-form rival over 8.5f here on handicap debut last time, when Risk Averse Rebel (now 2lb better off) was one and a quarter lengths back in third; further progress likely, particularly over this longer trip; contender.
runners.comment: Improved form to finish 2nd over 8.5f here on h'cap debut; this longer trip should suit

runners.name: Risk Averse Rebel
runners.spotlight: Has finished behind Bergamo Gold (who's now 3lb worse off for a length) over C&D and Pangbourne (2lb worse off for 1.25l) over 8.5f here on last two starts; has a chance, but hasn't always looked straightforward.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Bergamo Gold and Pangbourne but hasn't always looked straightforward

runners.name: Frustration
runners.spotlight: Beaten 14l or more in her three runs, two in maiden fillies' events at Newmarket and the latest (stable debut) a C&D fillies' novice; unexposed but will need to improve on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten, last time on stable debut over C&D; needs to improve on handicap debut

runners.name: Lux Aeterna
