runners.spotlight: Makes his handicap debut on first run since being gelded; hasn't shown a great deal in three AW races at up to 1m, but is unexposed and may improve now up in trip.
runners.comment: Handicap debut on first run since being gelded; unexposed and may improve now up in trip
runners.owner: The Trip To Syndicate And Partners

runners.name: Causing Problems
runners.spotlight: Strong puller who has shown ordinary form over 7f at Newmarket, Yarmouth and Lingfield; has since been gelded; interesting up in trip on handicap debut with a hood tried, but will need to settle better to progress.
runners.comment: Strong puller; modest form over 7f; gelded since; tries new trip with hood for h'cap debut

races.name: Read Meg Nicholls' Blog At betmgm.co.uk Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Ophelia Grace
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of 5f Southwell maiden on second start and soft ground may not have suited next time, but she found little on her handicap debut over C&D in November (3-1) which leaves a question mark hanging.
runners.comment: Won 5f Southwell maiden on 2nd start; found little on handicap debut over C&D in November

runners.name: Moes Legacy
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner, like her dam (for this yard) and two of the latter's siblings; four turf wins in 2025 seemed to push her high in the weights but she returned to action better than ever when winning over C&D ten days ago and going back up 3lb doesn't look too harsh; can go well.
runners.comment: Prolific winner; better than ever with C&D win latest; back up 3lb; can go well

runners.name: Saucy Jane
runners.spotlight: Early 2yo who was off the mark in a 5f maiden at Beverley last May; subsequently ran well in Listed company over this trip and a 6f handicap but not obviously progressive and needs to improve on her reappearance fourth at Kempton (6f) to have a winning chance.
runners.comment: Won 5f maiden last May; some good efforts later but likely needs improvement to win
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 16 And Jmr Partners

runners.name: Angel Numbers
runners.spotlight: Successful debut at Chepstow (5f) last May; that didn't help her handicap mark but she returned to action with a good second of nine over C&D this month to one who'd had a run; back up 1lb; can be involved again.
runners.comment: Won 5f debut last May; good 2nd on reappearance over C&D; can be involved again

runners.name: Nad Alshiba Green
runners.spotlight: Unraced on AW but she acts on most ground on turf and both siblings won on artificial surfaces; had some useful 5f form for Mick Appleby last summer but she can give herself plenty to do and may also need to improve to win off a highish mark.
runners.comment: Unraced on AW; both siblings won on artificial surfaces; may need to improve off this mark

runners.name: Nifty
runners.spotlight: Followed a promising debut with two 6f wins, on Polytrack and turf; minor efforts in first two handicaps but she finished much closer over 6f on this track in October and the return to 5f needn't hold her back; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins as 2yo; creditable show over 6f here in October; not ruled out back at 5f

runners.name: Layla Liz
runners.spotlight: Progressive front-runner in sprint novices as 2yo, winning two from three; lost nothing in defeat on handicap debut over C&D last February when giving best to one ahead of his mark and, despite the absence since, she may well give a bold show off the same mark.
runners.comment: 2-3 in novices; good 2nd to well-treated rival in C&D handicap last February; off since

runners.name: Sams Hope
runners.spotlight: Had a tremendous spell for new yard between September and January, winning five at 6f (two here) or 5f; her handicap mark has taken a battering but a recent second at Southwell suggests she's not weighted out of it; Ryan Kavanagh 2-2 on her and she can be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Five sprint wins between September and January; up weights; good 2nd off this mark latest
runners.owner: D Morgan And K J Parris

races.name: Always Gamble Responsibly At BetMGM Classified Stakes

runners.name: Lhebayeb
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins, the latest in a classified event this month in a refitted hood; failed to get to grips with the leaders here six days later but she's had a longer break this time; can be very competitive if race pans out.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins, latest this month; well held six days later; a longer break this time

runners.name: Star Of Atlantis
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins include one over C&D last May but form has ebbed and flowed since and little encouragement in her two runs since a break.
runners.comment: C&D winner last May; form has ebbed and flowed since; little encouragement since a break

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: Unraced here since 2022 and nearly all his AW runs at Newcastle; richly deserves a change of luck following close calls there (1m2f and 1m) on last two starts, slightly unfortunate on Friday; bold show likely.
runners.comment: Nearly all AW runs at Newcastle; close calls on last two starts, unfortunate on Friday

runners.name: Bossy Parker
runners.spotlight: All four wins at 7f, two on this track, but he lost out only narrowly over C&D in January (Lhebayeb third); found less over 1m at Newcastle (favourite) last month but is of more interest back at Wolverhampton.
runners.comment: Ran well in C&D classified in January; lesser Newcastle run since; can do better back here

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Made all when breaking duck in 1m2f classified event at Chelmsford last month on yard debut; has run well in defeat in two starts over the extended 1m1f here since and this lesser test may well suit (some of his best form has been at 1m); tongue tied on last two outings but not today.
runners.comment: Running well for new yard, broke duck over 1m2f last month; this lesser test may suit

runners.name: Pebble Dash
runners.spotlight: Maiden; no impression in sole 1m run, at Southwell in January, but eligible for this level now and stayed on well when only just failing over 7f at Newcastle recently; this longer trip looks worth a go; considered.
runners.comment: Maiden; strong-finishing 2nd over 7f at Newcastle latest; this longer trip is worth a go

runners.name: Wingstar
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who finished in the frame three times for Sam England, including when fourth to Llebayeb over C&D 14 days ago; sold 1,600gns since; now goes goes in a hood with work to do.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; in the frame for previous yard but work to do on C&D form latest

runners.name: Nutcracker
runners.spotlight: Not yet off the mark and while he ran respectably on a rare run beyond 1m, when third in a C&D classified event two weeks ago, he just kept on at the one pace; needs extra for the win.
runners.comment: Maiden; respectable third in a recent C&D classified; needs extra for the win

runners.name: Spinning Dancer
runners.spotlight: Winless as yet; some good AW runs in the second half of 2025, including over C&D, but well held on last two starts; others preferred.
runners.comment: Maiden; some good AW runs in the second half of 2025 but well held on last two starts

runners.name: Galette
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has raced mostly at up to 1m; back to form when third of ten in a 7f classified event here (first-time blinkers) in February but she's since run poorly at Lingfield; up against it at the weights.
runners.comment: Maiden; back to form when 3rd over 7f here last month; up against it at the weights

runners.name: Boomtown Lad
runners.spotlight: Still very early days but yet to show much, including on handicap debut here in February; on a very low mark and has lots to find on these terms; now visored.
runners.comment: Early days but shown little in five runs; has lots to find on these terms; now visored

races.name: Win Â£250,000 With BetMGM's Golden Goals Handicap

runners.name: Captain Pickles
runners.spotlight: Took a long time to get off the mark but he's got the hang of winning now, having been successful over C&D and 7f at Southwell in two of his last three starts in classified events (outstayed at about 1m1f in between); should make a good go of it back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Won 2 of last 3 classifieds, including C&D; should make a good go of it back in handicap

runners.name: First Encounter
runners.spotlight: His options have improved since eligible for classified events and he's 2-3 at that level of late, over C&D and 1m at Lingfield; needs more back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Made the most of the drop to classified level, winning 2-3 this year; more needed today

runners.name: Arlecchinos Rex
runners.spotlight: Hard to place since wide-margin 2yo win but he was back on a low mark when successfully stepped up to about 1m1f here this month; didn't see out that trip so well next time and improvement needed on earlier C&D form.
runners.comment: Successfully stepped up to 1m1f here this month; lesser run since; more needed over C&D

runners.name: Brinton
runners.spotlight: Has a very solid record on this track but she's run only once here since winning a C&D classified event last March under Rob Hornby; only just above her latest winning mark last May and there's a case to be made.
runners.comment: Very solid record on this track, C&D winner last March; just above latest winning mark

runners.name: Take The A Train
runners.spotlight: Modest for Ed Dunlop, including in handicaps, but there was some promise in his second behind an odds-on shot at Newcastle (1m) recently on second run for this yard; hard to weigh up that effort but he could improve.
runners.comment: Mostly well held but some promise when second at Newcastle (1m) latest; could improve

runners.name: Sceptic
runners.spotlight: Latest two wins here, at about 1m1f and 7f, both off this sort of mark; it's been a long haul this winter but he looked on the way back over C&D recently, behind a subsequent winner in a higher grade; in the reckoning.
runners.comment: Had a slow winter but looked on the way back over C&D latest and weighted to go well

runners.name: Princess Vivi
runners.spotlight: Minor form in a handful of starts at up to 7f and little indication from her finishing over 7f that the step up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Minor form at up to 7f; style of finishing suggests a stamina query now up in trip

runners.name: Wyvern
runners.spotlight: Course winner who has run well over C&D; multiple runs at Newcastle since November, winning over  1m and 7f, but he's flagged of late and a recent third of five there, beaten just over 4l, didn't point to any great revival today.
runners.comment: Course winner with C&D form; two Newcastle wins in late 2025 but he has flagged of late

runners.name: Morning Suit
runners.spotlight: 6f winner on Polytrack as 2yo but he's had a poor time of it since the summer of 2024, in handicaps and classified events, and has stamina to prove over new trip.
runners.comment: 6f winner on Polytrack as 2yo; modest form since summer 2024; stamina query over new trip

runners.name: Mooretown Lad
runners.spotlight: Turf winner at 1m1f in Ireland as a 4yo but he didn't get much out of a hurdling spell later; low-level form for new yard this winter until running well for a long way over the extended 1m1f here last month; might do better back over C&D off much reduced mark.
runners.comment: 1m1f winner in Ireland as 4yo; low-level form since but on reduced mark and better latest

runners.name: Like Magic
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m and 1m1f in Germany, on turf and sand, but it's been a right old slog in Britain; at least he's dropped 22lb since last May and any market move would be interesting on yard debut (left Max Young); tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: Won at 1m/1m1f in Germany; poor form in Britain but right down the weights; new yard
